meaning: vt.[律]列名在陪审名单,(为审理某一案件)挑选(陪审团)
examples: If a jury has been demanded, the first order of business at trial will be to impanel the jury.
It was nearly 4 p. m. and getting a jury would take time, so the judge called a recess and went out in the hall looking to impanel anyone available for jury duty.
Moreover, there are other trials coming along quickly behind it, and lawyers on both sides expect to be able to impanel juries that aren't influenced by this verdict.
